mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes. It simplifies the setup process by integrating Docker and Kubernetes into a single application, making it ideal for developers who want to quickly prototype and test their applications. However, it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource limitations and lack of advanced orchestration features.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step downloads the Docker Desktop application for Mac, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

```

> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy Docker.app to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step launches Docker Desktop and enables Kubernetes.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

sleep 30

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "K" using {command down, option down}'

```

> Launch Docker Desktop application.
> Wait for Docker to initialize.
> Enable Kubernetes in Docker Desktop settings.
commands: osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "K" using {command down, option down}'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Kubernetes is running correctly within Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl version --short

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the installed Kubernetes version.
> Verify that the Kubernetes node is ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This step removes Docker Desktop from your Mac. Note that this will delete all local containers and images, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

osascript -e 'quit app "Docker"'

rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

```

> Quit Docker Desktop before uninstalling.
> Remove Docker application from Applications folder.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades Docker Desktop to the latest version.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

open /Applications/Docker.app

```

> Download the latest version of Docker Desktop.
> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy the new Docker.app to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.
> Launch the upgraded Docker Desktop application.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to Docker Desktop.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes components may not be fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and check again; if still not ready, restart Docker Desktop.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker Desktop is installed correctly and kubectl is included in the installation.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes version mismatch error
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop is running an unsupported Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Check Docker Desktop settings and ensure Kubernetes version is set to a supported version.

**Affected versions:** <= 4.29

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved resource management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'kubectl get nodes' showing the node status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "K" using {command down, option down}'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
